parsed_review.summary: CP00-244 (RP07 search-core, risk A, 10 units, RP07.P02.M06.S06-RP07.P02.M06.S15, "Synthetic Fixture Set" middle rows) is a sound descriptor-only pack and passes independent read-only review. Identity and scope are consistent across the registry binding (SEARCH_CORE_CP244_PACK_BINDING), manifest, contract current_pack + appended historical_pack_bindings tail, validator script, and model tests. Deliverable distribution {security_audit:1, implementation:5, ui:3, claude_review:1} and micro-title {Synthetic Fixture Set:10} match the 10 manifest units exactly and are enforced by validateSearchCoreCp244Coverage. Descriptor-only and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced redundantly: freezeCp244Result forces all dispatch/write/persist/lock/audit/receipt flags false; validateSearchCoreCp244ServiceFixtureMidDescriptor asserts per-row descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false, plus targeted no-leak checks for audit hint/body, state-transition writes, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, db-row creation, validation-error detail, and review/approval route dispatch; and the contract projection is held to deep-equality with the freshly generated descriptor. Authority boundary is correctly preserved (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false); Hermes appears only as a deterministic packet/receipt-shape descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. production_ready is correctly false with status implemented_pending_review, so no premature enterprise-trust claim is made. Command-evidence records all expected validation commands at exit 0, readiness auth-preflight passed, and the review was conducted with read-only Read/Grep/Glob against a 2150-byte prompt containing no embedded diff/source. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two non-blocking P3 notes are reported.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts not yet generated at review time
findings.details: The manifest's required_pack_artifacts and per-unit evidence_refs reference docs/closeout-packs/cp00-244/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. This is consistent with the declared hardening_sequence, in which these artifacts are produced by steps that run AFTER the read-only Claude run (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection). The manifest correctly reflects this with status implemented_pending_review, production_ready=false, and pack_level_claude_review.status=pending with empty valid_review_receipts.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the remaining sequence steps generate this review's normalized result (claude-review-result.json), the adjudication, and the construction inspection, and that npm run closeout-pack:validate confirms their presence before the commit/closeout step.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected in-progress state at the review step; non-blocking. Downstream closeout-pack:validate gate enforces artifact presence before commit.

findings.title: changed-file-scope classifies the pack's own evidence dir as unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-244/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. The cp00-244/ directory is the active pack's own evidence directory (related to this pack), so also tagging it unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is cosmetically inconsistent. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved lists only the unrelated .docx and the c-ldip-01 file, so there is no boundary, real-data, or scope-integrity impact; the seven tracked modified files still align with active_pack_implementation_refs.
findings.recommendation: Tighten the scope generator so the active pack's own evidence directory is not simultaneously tagged unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to keep the changed-file-scope labeling unambiguous.
findings.post_review_disposition: Cosmetic metadata labeling only; non-blocking; does not affect scope integrity, boundaries, or the production_ready determination.
